theorem_title: Weak formulation of the Poisson problem via Lax–Milgram
theorem_statement:
Weak formulation of the Poisson problem via Lax–Milgram.

Let \(\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^d\) be a bounded domain with sufficiently regular boundary, and let \(f \in L^2(\Omega)\). Then there exists a unique \(u \in H_0^1(\Omega)\) such that
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u \cdot \nabla v\,dx
=
\int_\Omega f v\,dx
\qquad \text{for all } v \in H_0^1(\Omega).
\]
Moreover,
\[
\|u\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)} \le C \|f\|_{L^2(\Omega)}.
\]

nodes.title: Weak formulation of the Poisson problem via Lax–Milgram
nodes.informal_statement:
With \(U:=H_0^1(\Omega)\), define \(a(u,v):=\int_\Omega \nabla u\cdot\nabla v\,dx\) and \(\ell(v):=\int_\Omega f v\,dx\). Then there exists a unique \(u\in U\) such that \(a(u,v)=\ell(v)\) for all \(v\in U\), i.e.
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u \cdot \nabla v\,dx = \int_\Omega f v\,dx
\qquad \forall v\in H_0^1(\Omega),
\]
and \(\|u\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)} \le C\|f\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\).
nodes.informal_proof_text: Check the hypotheses of Lax–Milgram on \(U=H_0^1(\Omega)\): the form \(a\) is bilinear, bounded, and coercive, and the functional \(\ell\) is continuous. Then apply the Lax–Milgram theorem to obtain existence and uniqueness of \(u\in U\) with \(a(u,v)=\ell(v)\) for all \(v\in U\). Unwinding the definitions gives the weak formulation. The standard a priori estimate from Lax–Milgram yields \(\|u\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)} \le C\|f\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\).

nodes.title: Boundedness of the Dirichlet bilinear form
nodes.informal_statement:
For all \(u,v\in H_0^1(\Omega)\),
\[
|a(u,v)|=\left|\int_\Omega \nabla u\cdot\nabla v\,dx\right|
\le \|u\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)}\,\|v\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)}.
\]
Hence \(a\) is bounded on \(H_0^1(\Omega)\times H_0^1(\Omega)\).
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Apply Cauchy–Schwarz to the integral of \(\nabla u\cdot\nabla v\):
\[
\left|\int_\Omega \nabla u\cdot\nabla v\,dx\right|
\le \left(\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx\right)^{1/2}
\left(\int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx\right)^{1/2}.
\]
On \(H_0^1(\Omega)\), this is exactly the \(H_0^1\)-norm bound used here.
nodes.formalization_rationale: Clean local inequality for the Dirichlet form; straightforward but still useful as a reusable boundedness fact for the application node.

nodes.title: Coercivity of the Dirichlet bilinear form
nodes.informal_statement:
For all \(u\in H_0^1(\Omega)\),
\[
a(u,u)=\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx=\|u\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)}^2.
\]
Therefore \(a\) is coercive on \(H_0^1(\Omega)\).
nodes.informal_proof_text:
By definition of \(a\),
\[
a(u,u)=\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx.
\]
Using the standard norm on \(H_0^1(\Omega)\), given by the \(L^2\)-norm of the gradient, this equals \(\|u\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)}^2\). Hence the coercivity constant is \(1\).
nodes.formalization_rationale: High-yield formal node because the coercivity constant is explicit and feeds directly into the final a priori bound.

nodes.title: Continuity of the load functional
nodes.informal_statement:
For all \(v\in H_0^1(\Omega)\),
\[
|\ell(v)|=\left|\int_\Omega f v\,dx\right|
\le C_P\|f\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\,\|v\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)}.
\]
Hence \(\ell\) is continuous on \(H_0^1(\Omega)\).
nodes.informal_proof_text:
First apply Cauchy–Schwarz:
\[
\left|\int_\Omega f v\,dx\right|\le \|f\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\|v\|_{L^2(\Omega)}.
\]
Then use the Poincaré inequality on \(H_0^1(\Omega)\),
\[
\|v\|_{L^2(\Omega)}\le C_P\|\nabla v\|_{L^2(\Omega)},
\]
to obtain the claimed bound in terms of \(\|v\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)}\).
nodes.formalization_rationale: Most valuable local formal island: it packages the concrete estimate for \(\ell\), combining Cauchy–Schwarz with Poincaré, and removes a real analytic obligation in the Lax–Milgram setup.

nodes.title: Lax–Milgram application and a priori estimate
nodes.informal_statement:
Since \(a\) is bilinear, bounded, and coercive on \(H_0^1(\Omega)\), and \(\ell\) is continuous, the Lax–Milgram theorem gives a unique \(u\in H_0^1(\Omega)\) such that \(a(u,v)=\ell(v)\) for all \(v\in H_0^1(\Omega)\). Moreover,
\[
\|u\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)}\le C\|f\|_{L^2(\Omega)}.
\]
nodes.informal_proof_text: Invoke Lax–Milgram with the previously verified properties. Existence and uniqueness follow immediately. The norm estimate is the standard estimate from the theorem, with constant determined by the continuity bound for \(\ell\) and the coercivity constant of \(a\). This yields the claimed weak solution and stability bound.
nodes.formalization_rationale: Reasonable formal target if the library already contains Lax–Milgram in a near-ready form; otherwise better treated as an application wrapper around the local analytic checks.

edges.label: supplies
edges.explanation: Provides boundedness of the bilinear form required by Lax–Milgram.

edges.label: supplies
edges.explanation: Provides coercivity of the bilinear form required by Lax–Milgram.

edges.label: supplies
edges.explanation: Provides continuity of the right-hand-side functional required by Lax–Milgram.

edges.label: yields
edges.explanation: Applying Lax–Milgram and expanding the definitions gives the weak formulation and estimate.
